I am an IT Manager at SPF Group. We distribute Nike and Centauro products, and I oversee the technology.
Hi, I am Marcus. So, Daniela, what do you do?
I am an IT Manager at SPF Group, here in Sao Paulo.
Nice. And what does SPF Group do?
We distribute Nike and Centauro products to stores across Brazil. We work in retail.
And what is your main responsibility there?
I oversee all technology projects, and I lead a team of twelve people.
That is great. We implement similar systems in the US.
Perfect. I am responsible for the infrastructure too. We should talk more.
We distribute Nike and Centauro products in Brazil.
I lead a team of twelve IT professionals.
I am responsible for the IT infrastructure.

Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, here is a quick tip about talking about your job. When someone asks what you do, do not just say your title. Tell them three things. First, your role. I am an IT Manager. Second, what the company does. We distribute Nike and Centauro products in Brazil. And third, your main responsibility. I oversee all technology projects, and I lead a team of twelve people. Use simple, strong verbs. I oversee, I lead, I manage, we implement, we distribute. Notice the present simple, because this is true every day. I lead, not I am leading. And keep it short. A good job pitch is about thirty seconds. You set the scene, you say what you do, and you stop. Try it now. Say your role, your company, and one responsibility, in three clear sentences. Talk soon, Daniela.

Hi, my name is Marcus, and I work in retail technology too. Let me tell you a bit about what I do. I am a Systems Manager at a sportswear distributor in the United States. My company distributes running shoes and gym wear to stores across the country. My main responsibility is the IT infrastructure, so I oversee the servers, the network, and the store systems. I also lead a small team of five engineers, and we implement new software for the warehouses. The retail world moves fast, so every day is different. Sometimes I am in meetings, and sometimes I am fixing an urgent problem. I really enjoy it. It is great to meet another IT Manager who works in the same industry. So, Daniela, tell me, what do you do?
